repair_reason: The replacement lane's safety conclusions were sound, but its temporary source-key list did not match the accepted story-phase handoff. The controller repaired the graph against story-phase-decomposition.json and revalidated the canonical task index.

serialized_shared_write_sets.resource: operation registry, schemas, and compatibility manifest
serialized_shared_write_sets.reason: Tests, catalog registration, schema publication, and Kernel consumption converge on one authoritative contract surface.

serialized_shared_write_sets.resource: Application Kernel, lock ownership, runtime, and durable state
serialized_shared_write_sets.reason: Policy, journal, recovery, lifecycle, clean, and takeover share Kernel composition and state invariants.

serialized_shared_write_sets.resource: CLI adapter, router, and output envelope
serialized_shared_write_sets.reason: The operation map, schemaVersion 1 envelope, help, exit codes, and lifecycle routes share the public CLI boundary.
Dependency pins, MCP/build scripts, packaging allowlist, and lockfile resolution must update serially.
Trace tests, canonical instructions, installer migration, and final audits converge on one Skill tree and installer surface.

serialized_shared_write_sets.resource: Plugin generator, templates, compatibility identity, and generated layout
serialized_shared_write_sets.reason: Golden expectations, generator code, bundle output, evidence schema, and release claims consume one immutable generated layout.

parallel_batches.purpose: Collect Windows, macOS, and Linux core/stdio/bundle evidence in non-overlapping evidence roots against one immutable build.
parallel_batches.write_set_rule: Each task owns only its OS-specific evidence directory; shared artifacts and readiness aggregation are immutable during the batch.

join.target: Independent Windows, macOS, and Linux core/stdio/bundle evidence cells for one immutable build identity.
join.pass: Every required OS cell has an honest terminal status and no OS result is inferred from another OS.
join.recovery: Keep failed, blocked, unsupported, or stale cells exact; rerun only the owning OS task and do not edit shared registries to mask failure.

parallel_batches.purpose: Execute independent real Codex and Claude install/routing/lifecycle/takeover evidence after the OS join.
parallel_batches.write_set_rule: Each task owns only its host-specific evidence directory; bundles, manifests, shared state, and readiness aggregation are immutable inputs.

join.target: Separate real Codex and Claude install/routing/lifecycle/takeover evidence.
join.pass: Each host/version/OS/scope cell has its own real-host receipt and neither host substitutes for the other.
join.recovery: Preserve the passing host receipt, rerun only the failed host task against unchanged artifacts, and return to serialized generator tasks if a rebuild is required.

parallel_policy: Only PB-001 and PB-002 receive [P]. Shared registries, Kernel, CLI, package/lockfile, state/journal, canonical Skill, generators, and readiness rollup remain serialized.

tasks.md: JP-001 and JP-002 before dependent evidence tasks
